production_blockers: Full canyon DEM/lidar tiles are not downloaded or conditioned yet.
Full canyon aerial/satellite imagery and water/vegetation/rock masks are not downloaded yet.

A stitched Lees Ferry NHD HU8 source overlay, exact-graph Colorado mainstem candidate, preview metric stationing, fixed-width cross-section seeds, preview alignment diagnostic, and NHD-aligned water prior are attached, but river-mile stationing, accepted banks, sandbars, eddies, release-aware wet-bank classes, and rapid annotations are not clipped into a reviewed corridor workspace yet.

USBR Glen Canyon daily total-release context is attached, but unit/terms confirmation, hourly/subdaily release patterns, release-band review, and guide/oarsman review are still required before flow bands are final.
No field/social reference media has explicit game-use rights attached yet.
NPS access/publication source leads are now attached, but exact launch/take-out, camp/beach, sensitive-location, river-mile, permit-language, and screenshot publication review remain required.

Colorado River reference annotations and rights manifest are attached as link-only review scaffolds, but no item-level media rights, first-party capture, station geometry, or guide approval is recorded yet.

caveats: The downloaded dashboard/JSON did not include explicit unit metadata, so cfs is inferred and must be confirmed before final gameplay tuning.
Daily same-date comparisons do not resolve subdaily hydropower fluctuations, travel-time lag, tributary inflow, side storage, or downstream routing.
All release bands remain review-gated until guide/oarsman and hydrologist review confirm gameplay/visual thresholds.
pulled_artifacts.provider: Bureau of Reclamation Upper Colorado Region hydrodata

caveats: Features are selected by source-feature bbox intersection with the pilot bounds and are not clipped to the bounds.
This extract is not an ordered Colorado River centerline, not river-mile stationing, not accepted banks, and not sandbar geometry.
NHD 3DHP annual CONUS products were identified in the catalog but not downloaded because they are multi-gigabyte national packages; use them later when a tiled/local 3DHP extraction path is chosen.
Guide/oarsman review, river-mile references, NPS/GCMRC context, release-band wet-bank review, and Unreal CRS transform review remain required before gameplay authority.
pulled_artifacts.provider: USGS / The National Map NHD HU8 shapefile products

caveats: This candidate is derived from named NHD Colorado River flowlines only; it is not river-mile stationing or accepted bank/sandbar geometry.
No endpoint snapping was used. The exact graph is clean for this pilot, but the direction still needs river-mile, aerial, NPS/GCMRC, and oarsman/geospatial review.
The source features are bbox-intersected full features, so parts of the candidate can extend outside the pilot bounds.
Use this line as an editor/review overlay and future stationing seed only until visible water edge, release-aware wet-bank classes, sandbars, eddies, and access/publication sensitivity are reviewed.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim derived overlay from USGS / The National Map NHD HU8 shapefile products

caveats: Confirm station zero, downstream direction, and river-mile relation with NPS/GCMRC references, DEM slope, Reclamation/USGS flow context, and oarsman/geospatial review.
Replace the preview equirectangular transform with the selected canyon working CRS before metric authoring.
Compare station samples against NAIP water edge, 3DEP terrain, release-dependent wet banks, sandbars, camps/access sensitivity, and rapid annotations.
Use left normals only as cross-section seeds until reviewed banks, shelves, eddies, sandbars, side channels, and hazard polygons are attached.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim preview stationing utility from the Colorado NHD mainstem candidate

caveats: Fixed 200 m half-width is intentionally generous for Grand Canyon/Lees Ferry review and does not represent channel width.
Lines use preview stationing normals and inherit the direction, CRS, and river-mile caveats of the mainstem stationing candidate.
No imagery, DEM, hydrology, release-band, NPS/GCMRC, or oarsman validation has accepted these seed lines yet.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim cross-section seed utility from Colorado preview stationing normals

caveats: The diagnostic uses preview masks with a bounded channel prior, so agreement is not production acceptance.
Out-of-bounds stations and seed lines reflect the current Lees Ferry pilot bbox and full-feature NHD candidate extent.
River-mile stationing, accepted banks, sandbars, eddies, release-aware wet-bank classes, and oarsman/geospatial review remain required.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim preview diagnostic from USGS TNM NHD, USDA/APFO NAIP masks, and USGS 3DEP relief

caveats: The prior is drawn from the NHD mainstem candidate, so it agrees with stationing by construction.
It does not identify visible water, shelves, side channels, islands, sandbars, eddies, boils, holes, or release-dependent wetted width.
Do not replace source-derived water masks, reviewed banks, or solver cross sections with this prior.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim preview mask-prior utility from Colorado NHD stationing

caveats: These are production-import folder overlays for editor review, not accepted river-mile or route authority.
Bank lines are fixed-offset seed endpoints from cross-section review lines, not observed wetted banks or sandbars.
Cross-section lines remain review seeds and must not be used as solver cross sections until river-mile, bank, sandbar, release, and oarsman review pass.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim derived artifact from the Colorado NHD mainstem stationing and cross-section seeds

caveats: The USBR dashboard/JSON still needs explicit unit metadata and terms confirmation before final flow bands.
Daily total releases do not capture hourly/subdaily hydropower fluctuations, downstream routing, tributary inflow, or side storage.
River-mile travel-time review, sandbar/wet-bank masks, oar-line annotations, and guide/oarsman validation remain open.
The current 18000 cfs high-release planning band is not observed in water-year 2026 to date and needs separate high-release evidence.
pulled_artifacts.provider: RaftSim review artifact from USBR Glen Canyon daily total release context and Colorado planning flow presets

caveats: No NPS media, map tiles, PDF text, or route geometry are imported by this artifact.

Do not use this artifact for permit/legal advice, current operation claims, or final route package promotion without human review.

Reference targets cannot drive production art, water, gameplay tuning, or marketing until rights and guide review are recorded.
